tasks.label: 🚀 Install: Full stack setup
tasks.detail: Run setup.sh - installs brew packages, pulls Ollama models, starts services

tasks.label: 🩺 Status: Check all services
tasks.detail: Quick health check of Ollama, Qdrant, Redis, Postgres, and project config

tasks.label: 🐘 Postgres: Setup (create db and user)
tasks.command: psql postgres -c "CREATE USER qms_agent WITH PASSWORD 'changeme';" -c "CREATE DATABASE qms_agent OWNER qms_agent;" -c "GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE qms_agent TO qms_agent;"
tasks.detail: Create the qms_agent database and user. Run once. Update POSTGRES_PASSWORD in .env to match.

tasks.label: 🐘 Postgres: Start service
tasks.detail: Start the Postgres service via brew

tasks.label: 🐘 Postgres: Open psql
tasks.detail: Open a psql shell to the qms_agent database

tasks.label: ✅ Check: Migrations wired to journal
tasks.command: for m in drizzle/0*.sql; do t=$(basename $m .sql); grep -q $t drizzle/meta/_journal.json && echo OK   $t || { echo MISSING from journal: $t; exit 1; }; done; echo; echo All migrations wired.
tasks.detail: Every drizzle/*.sql must have an entry in meta/_journal.json or db:migrate silently skips it. Exits non-zero on a gap, so it can gate the reindex chain.

tasks.label: 💾 Drizzle: Run migrations
tasks.detail: Apply pending migrations to the Postgres database

tasks.label: 💾 Drizzle: Generate migration
tasks.detail: Generate a new migration from changes in src/db/schema.ts

tasks.label: 💾 Drizzle: Open Studio
tasks.detail: Open the Drizzle Studio browser-based schema inspector

tasks.label: 🧪 Smoke: Test data plane
tasks.detail: Verify tiers, services, query records, and database all work end-to-end

tasks.label: 🧪 Smoke: Test auth
tasks.detail: Verify password hashing, JWT, user CRUD, and refresh token storage

tasks.label: 🧪 Smoke: Test agent
tasks.detail: Run the agent graph end-to-end without HTTP. Prompts for a question.

tasks.label: 🧪 Smoke: Test tables (structured data)
tasks.detail: Verify type inference, SQL load, registry, and the structured query builder end-to-end

tasks.label: 🧪 Smoke: Test hybrid retrieval
tasks.detail: End-to-end: agent queries SQL via the data API and exact data reaches the answer

tasks.label: 🧪 Smoke: Test rubrics + draft schema
tasks.detail: Validate the three-category rubric loader, content hashing, and the draft_sets/documents/rounds/issues round-trip

tasks.label: 🧪 Smoke: Test live sources
tasks.detail: Validate live-source descriptors, registry round-trip, and that the endpoint never leaks into the discovery blurb prose

tasks.label: 🧪 Smoke: Test XLSX register write
tasks.detail: Unit-test the deterministic register merge: append, update, version-before-write, atomic rename, locked-file and column-mapping rejection. No LLM, no DB.

tasks.label: 🧪 Smoke: Test subject scoping + enumeration
tasks.detail: Project/collection resolution from document metadata, and registry ENUMERATION of a collection with coverage reporting. Fixes the table lane's silent under-coverage of 'across all X' questions. Needs Postgres only.

tasks.label: 🧵 Smoke: Test section validator (the cut panels)
tasks.detail: Deterministic gate before the LLM: validates a produced document section against its declared schema. Recomputes RPN, marks missing fields insufficient_evidence, rejects out-of-scale ratings, flags ungrounded values and fabricated cross-references. Pure - no LLM, no DB.

tasks.label: ⚖️ Smoke: Test rubric scoring (weighted binary + gates)
tasks.detail: Deterministic aggregation of per-criterion PASS/FAIL into weighted score, critical-gate decision, and approval outcome. Includes the anti-fabrication pattern pre-check (was EAR99, prior CDR). The LLM never sees the weights. Pure - no LLM, no DB.

tasks.label: 🪡 Smoke: Test recipe executor (stub handlers)
tasks.detail: The deterministic recipe interpreter end to end with stubs: DAG validation, step ordering, output threading, one custody event per step, gap -> reviewRequired, critical gate -> block, halt at human gate. Postgres only, no Ollama.

tasks.label: 🧥 Draft: Generate a DFMEA end-to-end (needs Ollama)
tasks.detail: REAL generation: runs the actual LLM through the recipe to produce a DFMEA failure-modes section, validates and judges it, and reports quality (rows, grounded vs insufficient_evidence, fabrication catches, rubric score, custody chain). Needs Postgres + Ollama.

tasks.label: 🔒 Smoke: Test SECURITY (label enforcement)
tasks.detail: Proves the filter EXCLUDES: a restricted chunk is never returned to a caller lacking the label, an unlabelled point is invisible to everyone, and the table lane obeys the same filter. Uses an isolated collection.

tasks.label: 📜 Smoke: Test custody ledger
tasks.detail: Append-only hash-chained custody ledger: chaining, tamper detection, the self-verifying dossier, and signature anchoring. Proves a modified record breaks the chain. Needs Postgres only.

tasks.label: 📜 Smoke: Test custody END-TO-END (HTTP /ask -> /custody)
tasks.detail: Full stack: POST /ask over HTTP, capture the correlation id, GET /custody/:id, and assert the chain contains run_started -> retrieval -> sql_query -> run_completed and self-verifies. Needs Postgres, Qdrant, Ollama, Redis.

tasks.label: 🧪 Smoke: Test identity + entitlements
tasks.detail: Validate the entitlement seam: domain-scoped labels, per-subject grants, fail-closed on every error path, and decision provenance

tasks.label: 📊 Langfuse: Start
tasks.detail: Start the self-hosted Langfuse tracing stack (web, worker, postgres, clickhouse, redis, minio)

tasks.label: 📊 Langfuse: Stop
tasks.detail: Stop the Langfuse stack (data preserved in volumes)

tasks.label: 📊 Langfuse: Open dashboard
tasks.detail: Open the Langfuse web UI. CONTROLLED TOOL: traces contain retrieved chunk text, so its access list is the top clearance list.

tasks.label: 📊 Langfuse: Tail logs
tasks.detail: Follow the Langfuse stack logs (useful while it boots and runs migrations)

tasks.label: 🔐 Auth: Create admin user
tasks.detail: Interactive prompt to bootstrap the first admin user

tasks.label: 🌐 API: Start server
tasks.detail: Start the Express API server on port 4000. Label enforcement OFF unless QMS_ENFORCE_LABELS=true.

tasks.label: 🔒 API: Start server WITH label enforcement
tasks.detail: Start the API with access-label filtering active. ONLY after a reindex has written labels - Qdrant excludes points lacking access_labels, so enforcing before labels exist blacks out the corpus.

tasks.label: 🔄 Reindex: Full rebuild from git (clear all + ingest)
tasks.detail: Atomic: clears ALL stores (SQL tables + registry + Qdrant) and re-ingests from the git repo. Runs migrations first - schema.ts and the physical database must move together.

tasks.label: 🔄 Reindex: Full rebuild from local QMS_FOLDER
tasks.detail: Atomic: clears ALL stores and re-ingests from the local QMS_FOLDER. Runs migrations first.

tasks.label: 📡 Ingest: Live-source descriptors only
tasks.detail: Register live-source descriptors from live-sources/ and write their discovery blurbs. The DATA is never ingested - only a pointer. Also runs as part of Reindex.

tasks.label: 🧹 Clear: All stores (no re-ingest)
tasks.detail: Clear ALL stores (SQL tables + registry + Qdrant vectors) without re-ingesting. Asks for confirmation. Normally use Reindex instead.

tasks.label: 💬 Ask: Query the RAG
tasks.detail: Send a question to the RAG and get an answer with citations

tasks.label: 🔧 Qdrant: Ensure payload indexes
tasks.detail: Create payload indexes on the existing collection: has_structured_table, has_live_source, source_path, tier, section_id, parent_section_id, access_labels. Without access_labels the authorisation filter cannot run.

tasks.label: 🌐 Open: Qdrant dashboard
tasks.detail: Open the Qdrant web UI in your browser

tasks.label: 🤖 Agent: Run draft
tasks.detail: Run the drafting agent (currently smoke test - will become real agent)

tasks.label: 🛑 Teardown: Stop services (keep data)
tasks.detail: Stop Ollama, Redis, Postgres, Colima, and Qdrant. Data preserved.

tasks.label: 💥 Teardown: Purge all data
tasks.detail: Stop services AND delete all data. Asks for confirmation.

inputs.default: what are the columns in the risk register

inputs.description: Question to send to the agent
inputs.default: what are the columns in the risk register
